---
title: "TEST_genetic_drift_sim"
author: "STAT 345"
output: html_document
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Installed Packages
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(shiny)
```

## Bottleneck Function
```{r}
# This function takes a population and removes an exact user-specified percentage of individuals.
# The individuals removed are selected randomly, but the total number removed is fixed.
# Inputs: population = a vector representing individuals in the population
#         pct_remove = the exact proportion of the population to remove (between 0 and 1)
# Outputs: a data frame with the surviving population and the percent that was removed
remove_random_pct <- function(population, pct_remove) {
  n <- length(population) # Gets the size of the population

  num_remove <- floor(n * pct_remove) # Calculates the exact number of individuals to remove

  remove_indices <- sample(seq_len(n), size = num_remove) # Randomly selects which individuals die

  result <- population[-remove_indices] # Removes the randomly selected individuals

  final_result <- data.frame(result = result, pct_removed = pct_remove) # Creates a data frame of survivors and percent removed

  return(final_result)
}
```

## Genetic Drift Simulation Function
```{r}
# This function simulates genetic drift over multiple generations, showing how allele
# frequencies change over time due to random sampling. A bottleneck can be applied
# at a user-specified generation mid-simulation.
# Inputs: population_size = integer for starting population size,
#         frequencies = named vector of starting allele frequencies,
#         generations = integer for number of generations to simulate,
#         bottleneck_gen = integer for which generation the bottleneck occurs,
#         pct_remove = exact proportion of population to remove at bottleneck
# Outputs: a data frame with columns for generation, allele, and frequency
drift_simulation <- function(population_size, frequencies, generations, bottleneck_gen, pct_remove) {

  alleles <- names(frequencies)

  # Build the starting population from frequencies
  pop_alleles <- rep(names(frequencies), times = round(frequencies * population_size))

  # Fix any rounding issues so population size stays consistent
  diff <- population_size - length(pop_alleles)
  if (diff > 0) {
    pop_alleles <- c(pop_alleles, sample(names(frequencies), diff, replace = TRUE, prob = frequencies))
  } else if (diff < 0) {
    pop_alleles <- sample(pop_alleles, population_size)
  }

  # Shuffle so alleles are randomly distributed
  population <- sample(pop_alleles)

  # Track current population size (changes after bottleneck)
  current_size <- population_size

  # Store generation 1 results
  results <- data.frame(
    generation = 1,
    allele = alleles,
    frequency = as.numeric(table(factor(population, levels = alleles))) / current_size
  )

  # Run the simulation for remaining generations
  for (gen in 2:generations) {

    # Apply bottleneck at the user-chosen generation
    if (gen == bottleneck_gen) {
      survivors <- remove_random_pct(1:current_size, pct_remove) # Randomly removes exact pct of population
      current_size <- nrow(survivors) # Update population size to number of survivors
      population <- sample(population, size = current_size, replace = FALSE) # Keep only survivors
    }

    # Randomly sample the population to simulate genetic drift
    population <- sample(population, size = current_size, replace = TRUE)

    # Calculate new allele frequencies
    freq <- as.numeric(table(factor(population, levels = alleles))) / current_size

    # Add results to the data frame
    results <- rbind(results, data.frame(generation = gen, allele = alleles, frequency = freq))
  }

  return(results)
}
```

## Run Multiple Simulations Function
```{r}
# This function runs the genetic drift simulation multiple times and combines results.
# Inputs: num_simulations = integer for how many times to run the simulation,
#         population_size = integer for starting population size,
#         frequencies = named vector of starting allele frequencies,
#         generations = integer for number of generations to simulate,
#         bottleneck_gen = integer for which generation the bottleneck occurs,
#         pct_remove = exact proportion of population to remove at bottleneck
# Outputs: a combined data frame from all simulations with an added simulation column
run_simulations <- function(num_simulations, population_size, frequencies, generations, bottleneck_gen, pct_remove) {
  results <- map_dfr(1:num_simulations, function(sim) {
    drift_simulation(population_size, frequencies, generations, bottleneck_gen, pct_remove) %>%
      mutate(simulation = sim)
  })

  return(results)
}
```

## Summary Statistics Function
```{r}
# This function calculates summary statistics for allele frequencies at the
# final generation across all simulations.
# Inputs: results = data frame from run_simulations() containing generation,
#         allele, frequency, and simulation columns
# Outputs: a summarized data frame with one row per allele showing
#          mean, standard deviation, fixation rate, and loss rate of final frequencies
summary_stats <- function(results) {
  results %>%
    filter(generation == max(generation)) %>% # Keep only the last generation
    group_by(allele) %>% # Split into groups per allele
    summarise(
      Mean_Final_Frequency = round(mean(frequency), 4), # Average ending frequency across all simulations
      SD_Final_Frequency = round(sd(frequency), 4),     # How much frequency varied between simulations
      Fixation_Rate = round(mean(frequency == 1.0), 4), # Proportion of simulations where allele took over completely
      Loss_Rate = round(mean(frequency == 0.0), 4)      # Proportion of simulations where allele went extinct
    )
}
```

## Shiny App
```{r}
# UI
ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Genetic Drift Simulation"),

  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      numericInput("pop_size", "Population Size:", value = 1000, min = 10),

      # Allele frequency sliders
      sliderInput("brown", "Brown Frequency:", min = 0, max = 1, value = 0.5, step = 0.01),
      sliderInput("blue", "Blue Frequency:", min = 0, max = 1, value = 0.3, step = 0.01),
      helpText("Green frequency = 1 - Brown - Blue. Must sum to 1 or less."),

      numericInput("gens", "Generations:", value = 50, min = 2),
      numericInput("sims", "Simulations:", value = 50, min = 1),

      hr(),

      # Bottleneck controls
      h4("Bottleneck Settings"),
      numericInput("bottleneck_gen", "Bottleneck Generation:", value = 25, min = 2),
      helpText("Must be between 2 and the number of generations."),
      sliderInput("pct_remove", "Percent of Population Removed:", min = 1, max = 99, value = 50, step = 1),
      helpText("The exact percentage removed. Individuals are still chosen randomly."),

      hr(),

      # Simulation selector dropdown (rendered dynamically after run)
      uiOutput("sim_select"),

      actionButton("run", "Run Simulation", class = "btn-primary")
    ),

    mainPanel(
      h4("Population Counts: Generation 0, Post-Bottleneck, and Final Generation"),
      tableOutput("final_table"),
      hr(),
      h4("Summary Statistics Across All Simulations (Final Generation)"),
      tableOutput("summary_table"),
      hr(),
      h4("Average Allele Frequency Over Generations and All Simulations"),
      plotOutput("drift_plot"),
      hr(),
      h4("Allele Frequency for Selected Simulation"),
      plotOutput("single_sim_plot")
    )
  )
)

# Server
server <- function(input, output) {

  # Reactive: runs all simulations when the user clicks Run
  # Inputs: all user-controlled inputs from the UI
  # Outputs: a list containing results, population_size, generation0, and bottleneck_gen
  simulation_data <- eventReactive(input$run, {

    population_size <- input$pop_size
    generations <- input$gens
    brown_frequency <- input$brown
    blue_frequency <- input$blue
    green_frequency <- 1 - brown_frequency - blue_frequency
    bottleneck_gen <- input$bottleneck_gen
    pct_remove <- input$pct_remove / 100 # Convert slider percentage to proportion

    # Validate that frequencies are valid
    validate(need(green_frequency >= 0, "Brown + Blue frequencies must sum to 1 or less."))

    # Validate bottleneck generation is within range
    validate(need(bottleneck_gen >= 2 & bottleneck_gen <= generations,
                  "Bottleneck generation must be between 2 and the number of generations."))

    start_frequencies <- c(brown = brown_frequency, blue = blue_frequency, green = green_frequency)

    # Build generation 0 population
    allele_counts <- round(start_frequencies * population_size)
    difference <- population_size - sum(allele_counts)
    if (difference != 0) {
      allele_counts[which.max(allele_counts)] <- allele_counts[which.max(allele_counts)] + difference
    }
    alleles <- rep(names(allele_counts), times = allele_counts)
    alleles <- sample(alleles)
    generation0 <- data.frame(individual = 1:population_size, allele = alleles)

    # Run simulations — bottleneck now applied inside drift_simulation()
    results <- run_simulations(input$sims, population_size, start_frequencies, generations, bottleneck_gen, pct_remove)

    list(
      results = results,
      population_size = population_size,
      generation0 = generation0,
      bottleneck_gen = bottleneck_gen,
      pct_remove = pct_remove
    )
  })

  # Dynamically render simulation selector dropdown after simulations run
  output$sim_select <- renderUI({
    req(simulation_data())
    sims <- unique(simulation_data()$results$simulation)
    selectInput("selected_sim", "Select Simulation to View:",
                choices = sims,
                selected = sims[1])
  })

  # Table: generation 0, post-bottleneck, and final generation counts
  # Inputs: simulation_data() reactive list
  # Outputs: a combined table showing allele counts at three stages
  output$final_table <- renderTable({

    generation0 <- simulation_data()$generation0
    results <- simulation_data()$results
    population_size <- simulation_data()$population_size
    bottleneck_gen <- simulation_data()$bottleneck_gen

    gen0_counts <- generation0 %>%
      count(allele) %>%
      mutate(Stage = "Generation 0", Count = n) %>%
      select(Stage, allele, Count)

    # Post-bottleneck counts from the selected simulation at the bottleneck generation
    bottleneck_counts <- results %>%
      filter(simulation == input$selected_sim, generation == bottleneck_gen) %>%
      mutate(Count = round(frequency * population_size * (1 - simulation_data()$pct_remove)),
             Stage = "Post-Bottleneck") %>%
      select(Stage, allele, Count)

    final_counts <- results %>%
      filter(simulation == input$selected_sim, generation == max(generation)) %>%
      mutate(Count = round(frequency * population_size),
             Stage = "Final Generation") %>%
      select(Stage, allele, Count)

    bind_rows(gen0_counts, bottleneck_counts, final_counts)
  })

  # Table: summary statistics across all simulations at the final generation
  output$summary_table <- renderTable({
    results <- simulation_data()$results
    summary_stats(results)
  })

  # Plot: average allele frequency over all simulations with vertical bottleneck line
  output$drift_plot <- renderPlot({
    results <- simulation_data()$results
    bottleneck_gen <- simulation_data()$bottleneck_gen

    ggplot(results, aes(x = generation, y = frequency, color = allele)) +
      stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1) +
      # Vertical dashed line showing when the bottleneck occurred
      geom_vline(xintercept = bottleneck_gen, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
      annotate("text", x = bottleneck_gen, y = 1, label = "Bottleneck", color = "red",
               hjust = -0.1, vjust = 1, size = 3.5) +
      scale_color_manual(values = c(brown = "brown", blue = "blue", green = "darkgreen")) +
      labs(
        title = "Average Allele Frequency Over Generations",
        x = "Generation",
        y = "Frequency",
        color = "Allele"
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
  })

  # Plot: allele frequency for the selected simulation with vertical bottleneck line
  output$single_sim_plot <- renderPlot({
    req(simulation_data(), input$selected_sim)

    results <- simulation_data()$results %>%
      filter(simulation == input$selected_sim)

    bottleneck_gen <- simulation_data()$bottleneck_gen

    ggplot(results, aes(x = generation, y = frequency, color = allele)) +
      geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
      # Vertical dashed line showing when the bottleneck occurred
      geom_vline(xintercept = bottleneck_gen, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
      annotate("text", x = bottleneck_gen, y = 1, label = "Bottleneck", color = "red",
               hjust = -0.1, vjust = 1, size = 3.5) +
      scale_color_manual(values = c(brown = "brown", blue = "blue", green = "darkgreen")) +
      labs(
        title = paste("Simulation", input$selected_sim),
        x = "Generation",
        y = "Frequency",
        color = "Allele"
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```
